articles.title: Supergenes Help Fish Evolve Quickly
articles.summary: Scientists found special parts of DNA called 'supergenes' that help fish evolve faster. In Lake Malawi in Africa, over 800 types of cichlid fish have developed from one ancestor in a short time. These fish live in the same lake but have different jobs: some eat algae, some hunt, and others sift sand. The supergenes are like toolkits that keep useful traits together, such as good vision or hearing, so fish can adapt to places like deep water or sandy areas. Researchers from Cambridge and Antwerp studied the fish's DNA and discovered flipped DNA segments that stop genes from mixing during reproduction. This helps fish keep winning combinations of traits, making evolution quicker and allowing new species to form even when they live close together.

articles.title: NASA Finds a Colorful Puzzle Inside Asteroid Bennu
articles.summary: Scientists from NASA studied a special rock from an asteroid called Bennu. They found that the rock is not the same all over. Instead, it has three different types of tiny spots with different chemicals. One spot has simple carbon stuff, another has minerals that form with water, and a third has nitrogen, which is important for life. This shows that water on Bennu long ago did not mix things evenly. The samples came from the OSIRIS-REx mission, which brought them to Earth safely. This helps us learn about how the solar system started and what ingredients for life might be in space. The study used very small tools to see details too tiny for our eyes.

articles.title: Tiny Scrolls That Make Batteries and Sensors Better
articles.summary: Scientists have made tiny scrolls from a special material called MXene. These scrolls are super thin, like 100 times thinner than a hair! They are better at conducting electricity than flat sheets of MXene. This helps make batteries store more energy and sensors work better. The scrolls are made by rolling flat flakes into tube shapes. They let ions and molecules move easily, like highways. This can improve things like wearable electronics and smart clothes. The scientists made 10 grams of these scrolls using a new method. They tested six types of MXene, and the scrolls are strong and good for many uses.

articles.title: NASA Goes to the Moon with Military Help
articles.summary: NASA is leading a mission called Artemis II to go back to the Moon with astronauts. The military, like the Space Force, Air Force, and Navy, helps keep the astronauts safe. They track the rocket, rescue astronauts if needed, and pick them up after the trip. The military also thinks space could be a place for battles in the future, so they want to protect U.S. interests near the Moon. Countries like China are also exploring space, which makes the military want to be ready. This mission shows how NASA and the military work together for space exploration and safety.

articles.title: A Big Rocket Launch in Florida
articles.summary: In Florida, many people are excited to watch a rocket launch called Artemis II. About 400,000 people are gathering on beaches and roads to see it. The rocket will carry four astronauts who will fly around the Moon but not land on it. This is special because it's the first time in a long time that humans are going back to the Moon. People like Amanda and Brenda are happy to be there, and shops are selling T-shirts and souvenirs. The launch might inspire kids to dream about space. It's a big event that brings lots of visitors and fun to the area.

articles.title: How to Help Older People Escape Planes Safely
articles.summary: When there is an emergency on a plane, everyone needs to get out quickly in 90 seconds. But older people might move slower and need more help. Scientists studied this problem using computer models of an Airbus A320 plane. They found that spreading older passengers evenly around the cabin helps everyone evacuate faster. This is because if too many slow people are in one area, they can block exits. The study was inspired by a law that wants better safety rules. It shows that new rules should think about older passengers to keep everyone safe during emergencies.
